_doc: Byte->Unicode map for the Sikkim Herald live-text Lepcha body font (named layout group: TT21B1O00/TT106DO00/TTDA7O00/TT70AO00, outline-identical across the 2021-11-19 / 2022-03-24 / 2022-04-07 / 2022-06-01 editions). Byte->glyphname is identity Latin (PDF /Encoding); the glyph OUTLINE is Lepcha. Derived by glyph-shape identity (NCC/IoU vs Noto Sans Lepcha + Mingzat) anchored on the alphabetic consonant structure and positional statistics, then round-trip-validated against the printed crops. See outputs/lepcha-map-derivation/.
_confidence: Consonants A..Z + lowercase conjuncts/SHA/WA + digits + post/pre vowels (c=O,d=I,f=OO,g=U,h=UU,i=E) and final signs are visually + round-trip confirmed (HIGH). Bytes NOT in this map are deliberately UNRESOLVED (do not guess): 0x5D ']' (544x, small superscript pre-base mark; isolated shape best-matches CONSONANT SIGN K (0.79) but its pre-base position is inconsistent with a final sign and forcing it to any vowel creates invalid double-vowel clusters -> identity unresolved); 0x25 '%' (224x, almost always after NUKTA 0x5C -> a NUKTA-companion glyph, identity unresolved); 0x2A '*', 0x2D '-' (likely literal punctuation), 0x28/0x29/0x2B/0x2F (rare). Lines containing any unmapped byte are excluded from faithful GT (clean gating). See outputs/lepcha-map-derivation/ for contact sheets + round-trip panels + the dotted-circle validity metric.
